mnemonics.title: Loop Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: WDoFEF: While → Do-while → For → Enhanced For - Remember: 'While Doing For Each Friend' to recall loop types from simple to advanced.

mnemonics.title: Break vs Continue
mnemonics.content: BREAK = BREAK out completely | CONTINUE = CONTINUE to next iteration - Think: Break = Stop the whole show, Continue = Skip this song, play next.

mnemonics.title: For Loop Structure
mnemonics.content: ICU: Initialization → Condition → Update - Remember: 'I See You' as the 3 parts of a for loop.

acronyms.expansion: While Condition Is Evaluated - Execute Body
acronyms.usage: Used when number of iterations is not known beforehand

acronyms.expansion: Do the body While condition is true (check at end)
acronyms.usage: Used when at least one execution is guaranteed
Used when number of iterations is known

acronyms.expansion: For Each Element From collection - Fetch element
acronyms.usage: Used to traverse arrays and collections without index

visualTips.visualization: Think of a circle/oval with condition at top, arrow going down through body, then back up to condition - if true, go through again.
A box with body at top, arrow to condition at bottom, then curve back up - shows body executes before condition check.
Three boxes in a row: init | condition | update - think of an assembly line where items pass through each stage.
A straight arrow pointing OUT of the loop box, bypassing everything else.
A curved arrow that jumps from inside body back to the top of loop, skipping rest of body.

analogies.analogy: Like checking if you have gas before starting a journey - check condition first, then act, repeat.
Like getting on a ride - you experience it first (body), then decide if you want to go again (condition check).
Like counting numbers on fingers - you know exactly how many times you'll do it before starting.
Like reading each book in a shelf one by one without counting - just move to next book each time.
Like the hour and minute hands of a clock - minute hand completes full circle for each step of hour hand.
Like emergency exit in a building - immediately exits the current structure.
Like skipping a song in a playlist - moves to next song without stopping the playlist.
